user_message: Just came out of territory planning with Tailspin Toys. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Tailspin Toys
Date: 2024-01-06
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on territory planning from our last session.

[00:07:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:12:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:16:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:19:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:22:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:27:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:30:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-Q trap (Decision-log format doesn't natively carry speaker-attribution metadata); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-Q failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-Q failure mode (Decision-log format doesn't natively carry speaker-attribution metadata)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF47-Q -- Decision-log format doesn't natively carry speaker-attribution metadata
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
